notes.resolve: Lite & Verified resolve % are REAL, from swe-bench/experiments (results/results.json, resolved/denom). Pro % are from the Scale SEAL public standardized set (morphllm.com/swe-bench-pro). Darwin entries are our own gold-graded runs on the official swebench harness. LiveCodeBench pass@1 is REAL, scored by the official lcb_runner codegen_metrics harness (private tests, never seen during generation).
notes.cost: Official entries' cost/instance is ESTIMATED from each system's DISCLOSED model(s) + scaffold shape × public token pricing — not a figure those teams published; order-of-magnitude only (marked 'est'). 'undisclosed' = no public model/cost. Darwin costs are MEASURED from actual OpenRouter spend.
notes.darwin: Darwin is measured on SWE-bench Lite (conformant: solver never sees gold tests in-loop). Verified/Pro runs are not done yet — those boards are shown for competitive context.
notes.livecodebench: Darwin LCB points: balanced n=100 SUBSET of release_v5's >=2024-11-01 window (post-cutoff for deepseek's disclosed cutoff, contamination-resistant). Difficulty mix easy31/med35/hard34, 53 AtCoder/47 LeetCode, 53 stdin/47 functional. Scored by the OFFICIAL lcb_runner codegen_metrics harness (eval validated end-to-end: known-correct stdin+functional->PASS, empty/wrong->FAIL). Two arms on the SAME instances: (A) single-shot deepseek-chat with a ROBUST extractor (prefers the largest python block that parses, not just the last fenced block -- fixes the empty-extraction misses); (B) a cost-cascade that escalates to deepseek-r1-0528 (reasoner) ONLY when the cheap base emits no code OR fails the PUBLIC example tests (hidden grading tests NEVER touched during solving). HONESTY: temp-0 is NOT deterministic on OpenRouter, so the raw 44->62 delta conflates the cascade lever with run-to-run noise. Attributable cascade lift (on the 27 escalated problems, the only DESIGNED difference): reasoner solved 12/27 vs single-shot's 4/27 = +8 net on the hard tail; the rest of the observed +18 is temp-0 variance on the 73 non-escalated problems. n=100 with a balanced (not easy-skewed) mix -- closer to the official ~34% full-set population than the old n=25; still not 1:1 (sampling + unpinned deepseek snapshot cutoff).
notes.terminalbench: Terminal-Bench resolve % are REAL, scored by the OFFICIAL terminal-bench harness (terminal-bench-core==0.1.1, 80 tasks): the harness builds each task's Docker container, runs the Darwin 1-tool (bash) ReAct shell agent inside it, then runs the task's OWN hidden tests — the agent NEVER sees or runs the tests (leakage-free by construction). Eval-validated: oracle agent → 100% PASS on hello-world (the harness scores a known-good solution correctly). Hardest-first ladder (hard→medium→easy). Darwin costs MEASURED from OpenRouter usage.cost. Cheap base = deepseek-chat (full-80); ceiling probe = Claude-Sonnet-4.5 on the 24-task hard band only. Small-n bands are directional (Wilson 95% CI shown). Some heavy ML/qemu tasks failed for HARNESS/ENVIRONMENT reasons (test runner produced no summary, or agent/test exceeded the 60-600s time budgets) rather than genuine solver misses — see entry notes. Official TB-1.0 leaderboard refs (Apex2/Ante/Droid 58-64.5% on Sonnet-4.5/Opus-4.1; Terminus baseline mean 34.7%) shown for context.
notes.swerebench: SWE-rebench (nebius/SWE-rebench, arxiv 2505.20411) is the DECONTAMINATED clean test (post-cutoff repos, continuously updated). Darwin entries are gold-eval'd on the official SWE-rebench fork harness (--namespace swerebench), §42-validated (gold resolves 51/65, empty 0/65). n=51 is a directional sample. The clean cascade (37.3%) lands below our contaminated Lite 51.3%/Verified 55.6% — consistent with contamination inflation, with the honest caveat that SWE-rebench is a different/harder distribution (drop is confounded, not cleanly attributable).

draco.label: DRACO — Deep Research (quality)
draco.metricNote: Different metric: 'Resolve %' here = DRACO research-quality × 100 (0–100), NOT bug-resolve. 'Cost' is per full corpus run (MEASURED, not estimated); Run total = that run cost.
draco.darwinNote: DRACO is our own cross-domain research benchmark. The cheap model beats the frontier on BOTH quality and cost — the harness/cost-router is the lever. All numbers measured (qualityPerUSD).

livecodebench.label: LiveCodeBench (n=100, release_v5 >=2024-11)

entries.name: Darwin · single-shot (robust extractor)
entries.note: 44/100 (easy 27/31, med 12/35, hard 5/34). OFFICIAL lcb_runner harness; eval validated. Robust extractor (largest parsing python block) cut empty extractions to 4/100. Balanced n=100 (not easy-skewed) -> harder population than old n=25's 64%. $0.0037/solved.

entries.name: Darwin · cost-cascade (->r1-0528)
entries.scaffold: cheap base + reasoner escalation on empty/public-fail
entries.note: 62/100 (easy 27/31, med 19/35, hard 16/34). Escalated 27% to the reasoner on public-test failure/empty extraction (hidden tests gold-held). Paired delta vs single-shot +18pp [7.9,28.1], McNemar p=1.4e-3. BUT temp-0 is nondeterministic on OpenRouter: ATTRIBUTABLE cascade lift is +8 (reasoner 12/27 vs base 4/27 on the escalated tail); ~+10 of the +18 is run-to-run noise. $0.0114/problem, $0.0185/solved.

entries.name: DeepSeek-V3 (official, full release_v5)
entries.note: Vendor-disclosed LCB pass@1 (08.01-12.01 window, whole set) -- full-set average over harder/older problems. Our balanced n=100 (44% single-shot) is closer to this population than the old easy-skewed n=25 (64%), but still NOT 1:1 (different window + sampling).

livecodebench.darwinNote: Balanced n=100 SUBSET of release_v5's >=2024-11-01 (post-cutoff) window, scored by the OFFICIAL lcb_runner harness (eval validated before trusting any number). Single-shot (robust extractor) = 44/100; cost-cascade (escalate to deepseek-r1-0528 on empty/public-test-failure) = 62/100. The raw +18pp is REAL on paired data (McNemar p=1.4e-3) but conflated with temp-0 nondeterminism; the ATTRIBUTABLE cascade lift is +8 on the 27 escalated problems (reasoner 12/27 vs base 4/27). Cascade costs ~7x more per problem ($0.0114 vs $0.0016) for the reasoning tail. n=100 directional; official ~34% full-set shown for context (different population).

entries.name: Darwin · cheap base (full-80)
entries.note: 17/80 full-80 conformant (official tb harness builds each task container, runs our 1-tool shell agent, then the task's OWN hidden tests score). By band: hard 2/24 (8.3%), medium 9/44 (20.5%), easy 6/12 (50.0%). Cost MEASURED from OpenRouter usage.cost. eval-validated (oracle→100% PASS).

entries.name: Darwin · Sonnet-4.5 (hard band)
entries.note: 6/24 HARD band ceiling probe (small-n, directional). 3× the cheap base's 2/24 on the same band: Sonnet cracks cartpole-rl-training, configure-git-webserver, git-multibranch, path-tracing, pytorch-model-cli.hard, swe-bench-astropy-1. CAVEAT: Sonnet FAILED organization-json-generator + prove-plus-comm (which the cheap base passed) due to 60s TEST-COMMAND timeouts (harness limit, not a solver miss) — true ceiling likely 8/24. Heavy ML tasks (pytorch/qemu/hf-model) also lost to agent/test timeouts.

entries.name: Darwin · GLM-5.2 alone

entries.name: Darwin · GLM→Opus cascade (coordinator)
entries.scaffold: GLM base → Opus-4.8 escalate on give-ups, conformant
entries.note: 19/51 decontaminated, gold-eval. DOWN from contaminated Lite 51.3%/Verified 55.6% — headline was contamination-inflated (partly) + SWE-rebench is a harder/different distribution (confounded). The shipped cascade, zero per-instance tuning.

entries.name: Darwin · Opus-4.8 alone (matched-15)
entries.note: 6/15 on the matched subset Opus covered before the +$60 cap (full-51 not run). On matched-15: Opus 40% > cascade 33.3% > GLM 20% — coordinator does NOT beat its best constituent on clean data; orchestration buys cost, not resolve-rate. n=15 directional.

entries.name: Darwin · conformant cascade (CVE seed)
entries.scaffold: open-loop single-shot, localize→search/replace, no in-loop oracle (conformant)
entries.note: 1/4 §42-eval-validated CVE-fix instances (base+test FAIL → gold+test PASS). Built from public OSV.dev CVEs + public OSS fix commits, SWE-bench format, FROM-SCRATCH per-instance Docker eval (no prebuilt images). deepseek 0/4, Opus 1/4 (psf/requests CVE-2023-32681 proxy-auth leak). Conformant: gold never applied during solve or grade. SEED — n=4 directional; cost = total probe $1.13 / 4. ADR-199.
